# Debt Buyer Rights - What Junk Debt Buyers Can Do [2026] - Debtbuyerrights
> What rights do debt buyers have? Learn FDCPA protections, how to challenge portfolio debt, and when to fight back. 2026 guide.

## About
debtbuyerrights.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Arbitration Clauses and Debt Buyers - Using Them Strategically - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/arbitration-clauses.html
- Challenging Debt Ownership - Making Them Prove It - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/challenging-debt-ownership.html
- FDCPA Protections Against Debt Buyers - Your Federal Rights - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/fdcpa-protections.html
- Debt Buyer Rights - What Junk Debt Buyers Can Do [2026] - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/
- Your Rights When a Debt Buyer Files a Claim in Bankruptcy - Complete Guide - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/know-your-rights.html
- Proof of Debt Requirements - What Debt Buyers Must Show - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/proof-of-debt.html
- Settlement Strategies for Debt Buyer Claims - Getting the Best Deal - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/settlement-strategies.html
- How Debt Buyers Use Shell Companies in Bankruptcy Court [2026 Guide] - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/shell-companies.html
- Statute of Limitations Defense - Defeating Old Debt Claims - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/sol-defense.html
- State Debt Buyer Licensing - Requirements and Consequences - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/state-licensing.html
- What Is a Debt Buyer? - How Your Debt Gets Sold - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/what-is-a-debt-buyer.html
- When to Fight a Debt Buyer - Knowing Your Leverage - Debtbuyerrights: https://debtbuyerrights.org/when-to-fight.html
- Privacy Policy: https://debtbuyerrights.org/privacy.html

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
